questions.question: Explain the difference between functional and non-functional requirements in IoT system design. Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Functional requirements define WHAT the system must do
Non-functional requirements define HOW well it must perform
Functional: sensor data collection, automatic actuation, alerts
Non-functional: response time < 200ms, 99.9% uptime, AES-256 encryption

questions.question: Describe the five layers of IoT architecture and briefly explain the function of each layer.
keyPoints: Perception Layer: Sensors and actuators for data acquisition
Network Layer: Communication infrastructure connecting devices
Middleware Layer: Data processing, storage, and management
Application Layer: User interfaces and industry applications
Business Layer: Analytics, business logic, and decision making

questions.question: What is edge computing in IoT? Why is it important for time-critical IoT applications?
keyPoints: Edge computing processes data near the source
Reduces latency by avoiding cloud round-trip
Improves response time for critical applications
Reduces bandwidth usage
Examples: autonomous vehicles, industrial control

questions.question: List and explain four important non-functional requirements to consider in IoT system design.
keyPoints: Scalability: Ability to handle increasing devices/data
Security: Protection against unauthorized access and data breaches
Reliability: Consistent performance and fault tolerance
Power Consumption: Critical for battery-operated devices
Latency: Response time requirements for real-time applications

questions.question: What is the role of a gateway in IoT architecture? List three key functions.
keyPoints: Protocol translation between different communication protocols
Data aggregation and filtering from multiple sensors
Edge computing and local data processing
Security enforcement and firewall
Local storage during network outages

questions.question: Compare MQTT and CoAP protocols for IoT communication. When would you choose each?
keyPoints: MQTT: Publish-subscribe, TCP-based, message broker needed
CoAP: Request-response, UDP-based, RESTful
MQTT: Better for reliable, bandwidth-constrained scenarios
CoAP: Better for resource-constrained devices needing low latency
Choice depends on device capabilities, reliability needs, and architecture

questions.question: What are the key considerations in selecting hardware components for an IoT device?
keyPoints: Processing capability matching application needs
Power consumption and battery life requirements
Memory (RAM/ROM) availability
Network connectivity options available
Sensor/actuator compatibility
Operating environment conditions
Cost constraints

questions.question: Design an IoT system for a smart parking application. Describe the system architecture, identify appropriate sensors, propose communication protocol, and explain how edge computing can be utilized. Include considerations for scalability and security.
keyPoints: Architecture: Perception (ultrasonic/IR sensors), Network (LoRaWAN/WiFi), Middleware (cloud), Application (mobile app)
Sensors: Ultrasonic distance sensors for parking spot detection
Protocol: MQTT for reliable message delivery, LoRaWAN for wide coverage
Edge Computing: Local gateway processes parking availability locally, reduces cloud traffic
Security: Device authentication, data encryption, secure API access
Scalability: Cloud microservices, load balancing, database sharding

questions.question: Explain the IoT system design methodology. Starting from requirements gathering to deployment, describe each phase and the key activities performed. Also discuss how iterative development benefits IoT projects.
keyPoints: Phase 1: Requirements Gathering - functional/non-functional requirements, use cases
Phase 2: System Design - architecture, component selection, data flow
Phase 3: Logical Design - DFDs, interface definitions, technology-agnostic
Phase 4: Implementation - hardware integration, software development
Phase 5: Testing - unit, integration, system, acceptance testing
Phase 6: Deployment - installation, configuration, monitoring
Phase 7: Maintenance - updates, troubleshooting, optimization
Iterative benefits: Early prototyping, flexibility to change, risk mitigation, continuous improvement

questions.question: You need to design an IoT-based industrial monitoring system for a factory floor with 100 machines. Each machine has 5 sensors generating 1KB data every 10 seconds. Answer: a) Calculate data generation rate per day b) Propose a data management strategy c) Recommend edge vs cloud processing distribution with justification
keyPoints: a) 100 machines × 5 sensors × 1KB × (3600s/10s) × 24hr = 432,000,000 KB = 432 GB/day
b) Data Management: Hot data (recent 1hr) at edge for real-time, Cold data (historical) in cloud for analytics, Data compression, Sampling rates adjustable
c) Edge: Immediate alerts, anomaly detection, local control actions (< 100ms)
Cloud: Long-term analytics, predictive maintenance models, Reporting
Justification: Edge for latency-critical, cloud for compute-intensive analytics, bandwidth optimization
